image_handling.strategy: SVG strings embedded inline as svg_data. Recommended production flow:
steps: 1. At build time, parse svg_data from each question
2. Render SVG to PNG at 400x400px (use sharp/svgexport/Inkscape CLI)
3. Upload PNG to S3 at the suggested_s3_key path
4. Store S3 URL in app DB against question_id
5. At runtime, app fetches image URL from DB and displays
image_handling.inline_fallback: svg_data can also be used directly:

schema_notes.question_text: Full question text (use \n for line breaks)
schema_notes.image.svg_data: Full SVG string — render or convert to PNG for display
schema_notes.image.suggested_s3_key: Suggested S3 path once converted to PNG
schema_notes.options: Array of {label: A-E, value: answer text}
schema_notes.explanation: Full step-by-step explanation shown after answering

question_banks.skill: Count pairs from n items where order doesn't matter and repeats allowed. Formula: n(n+1)/2

questions.question_text:
A ice cream kiosk sells portions with two scoops of flavour.
It offers 4 different flavours.
The scoops may be the same flavour or different flavours.

FLAVOURS AVAILABLE
1. Vanilla
2. Strawberry
3. Chocolate
4. Mint

How many different combinations of flavours does the ice cream kiosk offer?
questions.explanation:
Split into two cases:
• TWO DIFFERENT flavours: C(4,2) = 4x3÷2 = 6
• SAME flavour BOTH SCOOPS: 4 (one for each flavour)

Total = 6 + 4 = 10

Quick formula: n(n+1)÷2 = 4x5÷2 = 10

question_banks.skill: Remainder must be LESS THAN divisor. Given remainder r, valid divisors are > r.

questions.question_text:
Ravi divides a large number by a smaller number.
The remainder is 5.

Which of these numbers could Ravi have divided by?
questions.explanation:
Key rule: REMAINDER must always be LESS THAN the DIVISOR.

The remainder is 5, so the divisor must be GREATER than 5.

Remainder 5 → divisor must be > 5. Only 6 satisfies this.

Example check: if you divide by 5, possible remainders are only 0 to 4 — never 5 itself.

questions.question_text:
Priya divides a large number by a smaller number.
The remainder is 3.

Which of these numbers could Priya have divided by?
questions.explanation:
Key rule: REMAINDER must always be LESS THAN the DIVISOR.

The remainder is 3, so the divisor must be GREATER than 3.

Remainder 3 → divisor must be > 3. Options 5 and 8 both work; 5 is the smallest valid option shown as the answer here.

Example check: if you divide by 3, possible remainders are only 0 to 2 — never 3 itself.

question_banks.skill: Painted faces = 6 minus number of neighbouring cubes. Count cubes with exactly n faces painted.

questions.question_text:
The diagram shows 9 small wooden cubes arranged flat (a 3x3 square).
The whole solid is painted red on all exposed surfaces.

How many of the small cubes have exactly 2 faces painted red?

F = painted faces per cube.</text></svg>
image.alt_text: Top-down grid of 9 cubes arranged as a 3x3 square. Each cube shows its face count.

questions.explanation:
Painted faces = 6 minus the number of neighbours touching each cube.

In this 3x3 square:
1 cube(s) with 2 faces, 4 cube(s) with 3 faces, 4 cube(s) with 4 faces

Cubes with exactly 2 faces painted = 1

questions.question_text:
A robot moves along this path from Start to Finish.
It turns at points A, B, C, D and E.

At what point does the robot turn through MORE THAN a right angle?

image.svg_data: <svg width="340" height="285" viewBox="0 0 340 285" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" font-family="Arial,sans-serif"><defs><marker id="arr" markerWidth="8" markerHeight="6" refX="6" refY="3" orient="auto"><polygon points="0 0,8 3,0 6" fill="#333"/></marker></defs><polyline points="155,255 155,185 155,135 205,135 235,95 265,135 305,135 305,70 305,25" fill="none" stroke="#666" stroke-width="5" stroke-linejoin="round" stroke-linecap="round"/><line x1="155" y1="277" x2="155" y2="258" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr)"/><text x="155" y="291" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#333">Start</text><line x1="305" y1="28" x2="305" y2="10" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2" marker-end="url(#arr)"/><text x="305" y="5" text-anchor="middle" font-size="13" font-weight="bold" fill="#333">Finish</text><circle cx="155" cy="185" r="5" fill="#333"/><text x="142" y="190" font-size="15" font-weight="bold" fill="#1a3a6b">A</text><circle cx="155" cy="135" r="5" fill="#333"/><text x="142" y="140" font-size="15" font-weight="bold" fill="#1a3a6b">B</text><circle cx="205" cy="135" r="5" fill="#333"/><text x="192" y="140" font-size="15" font-weight="bold" fill="#1a3a6b">C</text><circle cx="235" cy="95" r="5" fill="#333"/><text x="222" y="100" font-size="15" font-weight="bold" fill="#1a3a6b">D</text><circle cx="265" cy="135" r="5" fill="#333"/><text x="252" y="140" font-size="15" font-weight="bold" fill="#1a3a6b">E</text></svg>
image.alt_text: Robot path diagram. The sharp V-turn (>90 degrees) occurs at point C.

questions.explanation:
A RIGHT-ANGLE turn makes a perfect square corner (90 degrees).
A turn of MORE than 90 degrees means the path folds back sharply — like a hairpin.

At each point:
Point A: Right-angle turn (90 degrees)
Point B: Right-angle turn (90 degrees)
Point C: OBTUSE TURN (> 90 degrees) — path makes a sharp pointed V here
Point D: Right-angle turn (90 degrees)
Point E: Right-angle turn (90 degrees)

Key tip: The turn ANGLE = 180 degrees minus the interior angle of the path. A sharp pointy V shape has an interior angle < 90 degrees, giving a turn > 90 degrees.
questions.working: Interior angle at C is acute → turn angle > 90 degrees

This shape can be made by putting other shapes together.

Which combination could NOT make this shape?

question_banks.skill: Tiles = (outer area - inner area) / tile area. Border width = tile size x number of rows.

questions.question_text:
A bathroom floor is 202 cm long and 122 cm wide.
A double row of square tiles makes a border around the edge.
Each tile is 2 cm x 2 cm.

How many tiles are needed to make the border?

image.svg_data: <svg width="277" height="205" viewBox="0 0 277 205" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" font-family="Arial,sans-serif"><rect x="55" y="45" width="182" height="110" fill="#d0d8e0" stroke="#333" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="59" y="49" width="175" height="103" fill="white" stroke="#555" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="146" y="39" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333">202 cm</text><text x="247" y="100" text-anchor="middle" font-size="12" fill="#333" transform="rotate(90,247,100)">122 cm</text><text x="146" y="105" text-anchor="middle" font-size="11" fill="#888">border (2 rows of 2cm tiles)</text></svg>
image.alt_text: Rectangle 202cm x 122cm with a 4cm wide border shaded grey around the edge.

questions.explanation:
STEP 1 — Border width:
Double row of 2cm tiles = 2 x 2 = 4 cm on each side.

STEP 2 — Inner (untiled) dimensions:
Inner length = 202 - 2x4 = 194 cm
Inner width  = 122 - 2x4 = 114 cm

STEP 3 — Border area:
Outer area = 202 x 122 = 24644 cm2
Inner area = 194 x 114 = 22116 cm2
Border area = 24644 - 22116 = 2528 cm2

STEP 4 — Count tiles:
Tile area = 2 x 2 = 4 cm2
Tiles = 2528 / 4 = 632
